company.name: Arkieva
company.tagline: Supply Chain Planning Software for Complex Enterprises
company.snapshot: Arkieva is a supply chain planning software company with 30+ years of experience providing demand planning, inventory optimization, and S&OP (Sales & Operations Planning) solutions. Founded in 1993 as Supply Chain Consultants and rebranded in 2011, the company serves complex manufacturers across process manufacturing, chemicals, food & beverage, and CPG industries. The company's core platform "Orbit" delivers advanced forecasting, inventory management, and supply planning with collaboration tools for enterprise resource planning at scale.
dz_fit_signals: Recent strategic growth investment from Banneker Partners (Apr 2025) signals aggressive expansion phase and GTM acceleration
New CEO appointment (Sep 2025) from i2 Technologies/Blue Yonder background indicates transformation toward cloud delivery and product modernization
Established enterprise customer base (Gates, Linde, Owens Corning, etc.) with 15,000+ users globally demonstrates proven sales model for scaling
Strong analyst recognition (Gartner Peer Insights, ranked 2nd in E2E MultiEnterprise planning) indicates growing market credibility and sales readiness

Active industry recognition (Top 100 Logistics & Supply Chain Technology Provider 2025, Demand Planning Solution of the Year at SupplyTech Breakthrough Awards 2025) shows momentum and market expansion
dz_fit_risks: Enterprise sales-led motion may require extended deal cycles and complex buying committees - traditional outbound SDR model may face longer sales cycles
Self-funded company history suggests conservative approach to sales spending - may be skeptical of aggressive outbound spend until newly-appointed CEO establishes GTM strategy
Deep technical product may deter or delay purchasing from buyers unfamiliar with advanced planning concepts - requires strong internal champion and procurement alignment

funding.revenue_growth_signal: Private equity growth capital indicates strong revenue performance and investor confidence in scaling cloud delivery and product modernization roadmap.

product.primary_offering: Advanced supply chain planning platform anchored by "Orbit" (in-memory planning engine) with "Beacon" collaboration tools for demand planning, inventory optimization, supply planning, and S&OP governance.
use_cases: Demand forecasting and collaborative planning across cross-functional teams
Inventory optimization and safety stock determination
Production scheduling and supply planning for complex manufacturing
Campaign and blend planning for process manufacturers
S&OP governance and integrated business planning
Supply chain agility and demand-driven response
problems_solved: Forecast accuracy improvement through ML-driven demand sensing
Safety stock optimization to reduce carrying costs
Complex manufacturing constraints (shelf life, quality, yield variability, campaign planning)
Cross-functional collaboration gaps in S&OP processes
Inability to respond rapidly to demand and supply changes
Manual or fragmented planning across disparate systems
replaces: Point solutions (standalone forecasting, inventory management tools)
Outdated APS systems lacking modern UI and collaboration capabilities

go_to_market.sales_motion_detail: Enterprise direct sales model focused on large manufacturers and complex process companies. New CEO appointment and Banneker Partners investment signal shift toward accelerated cloud GTM and likely increased sales hiring/outbound motion. Currently leverages established brand and customer success stories but likely building scaled sales infrastructure.
expansion_signals: New CEO (Anand Iyer) hired from i2/Blue Yonder background in Sep 2025 - signals cloud-first strategy and potential GTM acceleration
Banneker Partners investment (Apr 2025) explicitly focused on geographic expansion and product modernization
Recent major client wins: Wells Enterprises (Jun 2024), Tata Consumer Products (Dec 2024) - signals expanding presence in CPG and emerging markets
Gartner analyst recognition and Top 100 Supply Chain Technology Provider ranking (2025) signal market-share expansion efforts

differentiators: Purpose-built for process manufacturing complexity (shelf life, yield variability, campaign planning) vs. generic APS systems
Deep vertical expertise in discrete and process manufacturing with 30+ years of customer success stories
Collaborative S&OP governance tools (Beacon) designed for cross-functional alignment
In-memory optimization engine (Orbit) for speed and scenario modeling
Proven customer base includes industry leaders (Gates, Linde, Owens Corning, SPI Pharma, Sunsweet) demonstrating ROI
analyst_recognition: Gartner Peer Insights: Ranked 2nd in E2E MultiEnterprise planning, 3rd in Supply Planning Use Case (2024 Critical Capabilities Report)
Inbound Logistics: Top 100 Logistics and Supply Chain Technology Provider (2025)
SupplyTech Breakthrough Awards: Demand Planning Solution of the Year (2025)

review_themes_positive: Accurate demand forecasting and improved forecast accuracy
Strong collaboration features for cross-functional S&OP processes
Improvement in sales and revenue since adoption
Ability to create centralized demand plans enabling proactive market response
Excellent customer support and responsive implementation team
review_themes_negative: Complex user interface requiring specific expertise to navigate
Steep learning curve for end users
Remote desktop version performance could be improved
notable_case_studies: Wells Enterprises - End-to-end supply chain planning for largest privately held ice cream manufacturer in North America (2024-2025)
Tata Consumer Products - Tea blend optimization through Arkieva supply planning (Dec 2024)

news.headline: Arkieva Recognized as Top 100 Logistics and Supply Chain Technology Provider
news.summary: Arkieva was recognized by Inbound Logistics as a Top 100 Logistics and Supply Chain Technology Provider for 2025, continuing recognition from the 2024 edition.
news.relevance_to_dz: Analyst and industry recognition signals growing market momentum and credibility - ideal proof point for DZ positioning around market leadership and customer trust.

news.headline: Arkieva Announces Strategic Growth Investment from Banneker Partners
news.summary: Arkieva announced a significant growth investment from Banneker Partners (PE firm) to accelerate product innovation, cloud delivery modernization, and geographic expansion.
news.relevance_to_dz: Growth capital signals aggressive GTM expansion and likely increased sales hiring - strong signal that Arkieva is in expansion mode and may have budget/headcount priorities shifting toward outbound pipeline generation.

news.headline: Arkieva Names New CEO Anand Iyer
news.summary: Arkieva appointed Anand Iyer as Chief Executive Officer, bringing 30 years of industry experience including leadership roles at i2 Technologies and Serus (now e2Open). Leadership change signals acceleration agenda toward cloud delivery and product modernization.
news.relevance_to_dz: New CEO from i2/Blue Yonder background indicates GTM transformation and likely sales infrastructure buildout - timing suggests Arkieva entering aggressive growth phase where DZ can position scaled outbound pipeline support.

news.headline: Wells Enterprises End-to-End Supply Chain Planning Implementation
news.summary: Arkieva announced successful implementation of comprehensive supply chain planning optimization for Wells Enterprises, North America's largest privately held ice cream manufacturer, to handle production complexity and support business growth.
news.relevance_to_dz: Major customer win demonstrates strong product-market fit in CPG/food manufacturing vertical and execution capability - proof point for sales team's ability to land flagship accounts.

buyer.primary_persona: Supply Chain Director or VP of Planning and Scheduling responsible for enterprise-wide demand planning, S&OP governance, and supply chain optimization. Owns budget for planning software and drives process improvement initiatives.
buyer.economic_buyer: Chief Operating Officer (COO), Chief Supply Chain Officer (CSCO), or VP of Operations who controls budget for supply chain transformation and process improvement technology.
typical_buying_committee: CFO or Finance Business Partner (ROI validation)
persona_pain_points: Forecast accuracy falling short, leading to safety stock buildup or stockouts
Disconnected demand/supply planning across functions (sales, operations, procurement)
Manual or spreadsheet-driven S&OP processes slowing decision velocity
Inability to respond to demand disruptions or market shifts quickly
Complexity of production constraints (shelf life, quality, yield) not captured in existing systems
Limited visibility into supply risk and alternative scenarios
persona_goals: Reduce safety stock and carrying costs
Accelerate S&OP decision cycles from monthly to weekly or bi-weekly
Enable cross-functional collaboration on demand and supply alignment
Support business growth without proportional increase in planning headcount
what_they_care_about: ROI and payback period (3-18 months typical for manufacturing software)
Vendor stability and customer success track record
Depth of vertical expertise (process manufacturing nuances)
Scalability as company grows or expands into new markets
what_they_dont_care_about: Generic marketing messaging or competitive positioning
Features outside supply chain scope (CRM, HCM integration)
Flashy UI lacking substantive planning capability

likely_objections.objection: We already have planning built into our ERP (SAP/Oracle/Infor). Why replace it?
likely_objections.reframe: Enterprise ERPs provide foundational planning but lack the advanced optimization, scenario modeling, and cross-functional governance tools built into purpose-designed APS. Arkieva layers on specialized algorithms and collaboration that drive 5-15% improvement in forecast accuracy and safety stock reduction at scale.
likely_objections.evidence: Arkieva customer base (Gates, Linde, Owens Corning) are all large enterprises running major ERPs but chose Arkieva for specialized planning depth. Gartner Peer Insights recognizes Arkieva as ranking 2nd in E2E MultiEnterprise planning.

likely_objections.objection: Implementation risk is too high. We've been burned by planning software before.
likely_objections.reframe: Arkieva has 30+ years of customer success in complex manufacturing. Their implementation team has deep vertical expertise and proven playbooks for phased rollouts. Reference customers (Wells Enterprises ice cream, Tata Consumer tea) show rapid time-to-value even for complex, multi-facility operations.
likely_objections.evidence: Wells Enterprises and Tata Consumer Products recent case studies (2024-2025) demonstrate successful large-scale implementations in highly complex manufacturing environments.

likely_objections.objection: We're concerned about moving to cloud. Our legacy systems are on-prem and we want to maintain control.
likely_objections.reframe: Arkieva supports both on-prem and cloud deployment options. New leadership and Banneker Partners investment focused specifically on cloud modernization but with flexible deployment. Talk to their sales team about your architecture preferences—enterprise customers have options.
likely_objections.evidence: New CEO Anand Iyer from i2/Blue Yonder background signals cloud-first strategy but legacy on-prem customers remain supported. Banneker investment explicitly mentions cloud delivery acceleration.

likely_objections.objection: We're small/mid-market. Arkieva seems built for large enterprises. Is the solution right-sized for us?
likely_objections.reframe: Arkieva serves the full enterprise spectrum. Their platform Orbit supports on-prem or cloud, and modules are modular—start with demand planning or inventory optimization and scale. They've implemented in mid-market manufacturing (100-500 employee operations) as well as Fortune 500.
likely_objections.evidence: Customer diversity from Escalade Sports to Owens Corning to SPI Pharma shows breadth of company sizes served. Sales FAQ on website explicitly addresses SMB/small business questions.

sdr_team_signals: No specific evidence of dedicated SDR team found in public job postings or LinkedIn
Traditional sales-led enterprise model typically supported by account executives and presales
sales_hiring_signals: New CEO appointment (Sep 2025) from i2 background signals likely sales infrastructure buildout
Banneker Partners investment (Apr 2025) explicitly targets GTM acceleration and product innovation - typically includes sales team expansion
Recent customer wins (Wells, Tata) and Top 100 analyst recognition suggest sales team is active but may be bottleneck as inbound demand grows
outbound_intelligence.outbound_maturity_rationale: Arkieva has 30+ years of proven sales execution in enterprise accounts but limited evidence of systematic SDR/BDR outbound motion. With new CEO from Blue Yonder background and PE backing, company is likely in transition from inbound/customer success motion toward scaled outbound—but this infrastructure is likely still being built.

leadership.name: Anand Iyer
leadership.title: Chief Executive Officer
leadership.note: Appointed Sep 2025. Former VP at i2 Technologies (now Blue Yonder) and Serus (now e2Open). 30+ years of supply chain software industry experience. Brought in to lead cloud modernization and GTM acceleration.

leadership.name: Harpal Singh
leadership.title: Founder
leadership.note: Founded Supply Chain Consultants (now Arkieva) in 1993. Background as analytics consultant at DuPont. Led company's evolution from consulting to software product company (Zemeter, then Arkieva platform).

leadership.name: Sujit Singh
leadership.title: Chief Operating Officer (COO)
leadership.note: Manages operations and likely key stakeholder in GTM strategy execution under new CEO.

leadership.name: Georgi Genov
leadership.title: Chief Technology Officer (CTO)
leadership.note: Responsible for technical strategy and product development, including cloud modernization roadmap.

contacts.name: Parth Thakker
contacts.title: Chief Commercial Officer
contacts.profile_summary: Sales leader with 15+ years in enterprise software and supply chain technology. Extensive experience building and scaling global sales teams for AI-driven supply chain and inventory solutions. Founded/early employee experience at antuit.ai (acquired by Zebra Technologies 2021), where he built go-to-market organization from ground up and scaled from startup to mid-market revenue leader. Background spans sales leadership, partnerships, customer success, and solution consulting. MBA from NYU Stern; Engineering background (Stevens Institute). Strong track record in manufacturing, CPG, and retail verticals with emphasis on supply chain optimization solutions.
contacts.role_description: Chief Commercial Officer—responsible for all go-to-market functions including sales, customer success, partnerships, and commercial strategy
likely_pain_points: Scaling sales team while maintaining quality and culture during rapid growth phase
Building SDR/BDR motion from scratch or scaling existing outbound capability
Aligning new CEO's transformation agenda with sales organization and Banneker Partners' growth expectations
Capturing market share in supply chain planning category against entrenched ERP and APS competitors
Developing sales messaging for technical product (advanced planning) targeting non-technical buying committees
likely_goals: Build sales infrastructure to support geographic expansion and new product lines
Establish outbound pipeline to complement inbound and customer success motion
Achieve ARR targets aligned with Banneker Partners' investment thesis
Develop competitive positioning against SAP, Blue Yonder, and other competitors
Scale customer success and retention alongside new customer acquisition
contacts.buyer_influence: Economic buyer and final decision-maker on sales tool/service investments
contacts.note_on_prior_employment: Based on research, Parth Thakker's background includes antuit.ai (supply chain AI) and Zebra Technologies (enterprise inventory solutions). antuit.ai is likely a PEER or ADJACENT match if in DZ client database under different name (check for Zebra, antuit, inventory solutions vendors).

prior_employment.position: SVP Global Head of Sales / Principal
prior_employment.dz_match: FOUND: Zebra Technologies (Domain: Data & Analytics, Category: Analytics and Business Intelligence Platforms, Program: Both)
prior_employment.relevance: Built global sales organization for AI-driven supply chain software serving manufacturers and CPG. Highly relevant to Arkieva GTM model and similar buyer personas.

prior_employment.dz_match: Genpact may be in DZ database as services vendor - verify
prior_employment.relevance: Digital transformation services background relevant to enterprise sales and customer success at scale.

dz_positioning.recommended_pitch_angle: Help Arkieva's newly appointed CEO accelerate outbound pipeline and sales team scaling as they leverage Banneker Partners capital for cloud-driven GTM expansion.
dz_positioning.opening_question: With your new CEO from Blue Yonder coming in, and Banneker Partners backing you for cloud modernization, what does your sales team infrastructure and outbound motion look like right now—are you building that out from scratch or extending an existing model?
discovery_questions: How is the new leadership team planning to scale sales capacity to capture the market opportunity that Banneker Partners is funding?
What's your current mix between inbound/existing customer success pipeline versus outbound demand generation?
Are you planning to build a dedicated SDR/BDR team to support your geographic expansion and product modernization roadmap?

sales_angle_points.title: CEO-Driven Transformation & Sales Scaling
sales_angle_points.body: Your new CEO Anand Iyer comes from i2/Blue Yonder with 30+ years of enterprise software GTM experience. Banneker Partners invested specifically to accelerate cloud delivery and expand geographically. This is a classic CEOtransformation moment where sales infrastructure buildout is critical to capturing the investment's value. DZ helps ambitious teams like yours generate consistent pipeline while your sales leaders set up the long-term playbooks.
sales_angle_points.supporting_data: Anand Iyer appointment Sep 2025; Banneker investment Apr 2025; CEO background in scaling enterprise sales at i2 (now Blue Yonder)

sales_angle_points.title: Enterprise Customer Win Momentum
sales_angle_points.body: You've proven major wins in complex manufacturing (Wells Enterprises ice cream, Tata Consumer tea)—both in 2024. This demonstrates that your solution resonates with enterprise buyers. The gap now is systematic demand generation to feed that sales team. DZ's outbound playbooks are built for this: consistent pipeline to top-tier manufacturers in your core verticals.
sales_angle_points.supporting_data: Wells Enterprises (Jun 2024), Tata Consumer Products (Dec 2024), Gartner recognition (2nd in E2E MultiEnterprise planning)

sales_angle_points.title: Vertical Expertise Unlocks Differentiation
sales_angle_points.body: Your unique competitive advantage is deep vertical expertise in process manufacturing—shelf life, yield variability, campaign planning. That's hard to explain at scale without deliberate, targeted outbound. DZ helps you reach process manufacturing leaders (chemical, pharma, food & beverage, industrials) with messaging that resonates with their specific constraints.
sales_angle_points.supporting_data: Customer base (Escalade Sports, Gates, Linde, Owens Corning, SPI Pharma, Sunsweet) all in process-heavy industries; Gartner recognition for supply planning and E2E planning use cases

sales_angle_points.title: Analyst Momentum as Sales Proof Points
sales_angle_points.body: You're on Gartner Peer Insights (2nd in E2E planning), Inbound Logistics Top 100 (2025), SupplyChainBrain's Top 50 partners, and SupplyTech Breakthrough's Demand Planning Solution of the Year. These are gold for sales conversations—they prove market leadership. DZ amplifies these proof points through targeted outbound to decision-makers in your target verticals.
sales_angle_points.supporting_data: Gartner Peer Insights 2024 Critical Capabilities (2nd E2E, 3rd Supply Planning); Inbound Logistics Top 100; SupplyChainBrain Top 50; SupplyTech Breakthrough 2025 award

proof_points.claim: Enterprise software scale requires systematic outbound pipeline, not just inbound momentum.
proof_points.evidence: New CEO's background at i2/Blue Yonder (scaling from regional to global) and Banneker Partners' playbook emphasize build-scale-repeat model. Pure inbound/customer success won't capture Banneker's growth thesis.

proof_points.claim: DZ delivers consistent pipeline to target verticals—process manufacturing, chemicals, pharma, F&B—where your vertical expertise is defensible.
proof_points.evidence: DZ's outbound methodology reaches and qualifies buyers in your exact verticals. Your customers (Gates, Linde, Owens Corning) are DZ account types; DZ can systematically reach their peers.

proof_points.claim: Analyst recognition and customer wins are powerful sales conversation starters—but only if sales team hears about them systematically.
proof_points.evidence: DZ amplifies proof points through targeted outbound. Your Gartner ranking, Top 100, and awards are assets that multiply in value when delivered strategically to the right prospects.

red_flags: New CEO and organizational change may slow decision-making in next 90 days—validate sponsorship early and identify who owns sales hiring/outbound GTM budget
Banneker Partners investment may have strict revenue/pipeline targets—understand their timeline expectations to position DZ as accelerator vs. cost
Enterprise sales cycles are long (6-12 months typical)—ensure Arkieva views outbound as lead generation for pipeline, not immediate bookings
dz_positioning.next_steps_recommendation: Arrange call with new CEO (Anand Iyer) and Chief Revenue Officer or VP Sales to understand sales infrastructure roadmap and Banneker-backed growth targets. Position DZ as tactical pipeline accelerator while they build long-term GTM organization.

cliff_notes.one_liner: Arkieva is a maturing supply chain planning software leader in growth phase (new CEO, PE investment) targeting complex manufacturers with enterprise APS solutions—prime prospect for DZ outbound support as they scale sales infrastructure.
cliff_notes.most_important_signal: Zero direct client matches in DZ database (supply chain planning not in historical focus), but massive DZ opportunity: new CEO + PE backing signals Arkieva entering high-velocity sales scaling phase where systematic outbound pipeline is critical.
cliff_notes.most_likely_objection: We already have planning built into our ERP—why replace it?
cliff_notes.single_best_angle: CEO-Driven Transformation & Sales Scaling – Help new leadership team accelerate outbound pipeline as they execute Banneker Partners' cloud modernization and geographic expansion thesis
cliff_notes.watch_out: Enterprise sales cycle length and deal complexity may require longer engagement models. Verify that new CEO's expansion agenda includes outbound/demand generation headcount (vs. purely product/expansion-driven spending).
